intent.raw: Create a subagent strategy for deep research comparing whole Arcanum and frumu-ai/tandem, including how they could interact and what approaches are complementary or divergent.
intent.objective: Plan a governed multi-agent research investigation comparing the whole Arcanum repository with frumu-ai/tandem, focused on system identity, capability/workflow models, runtime enforcement, memory/evidence, governance, and integration architecture.
intent.operator_sentence: Use task-session to repair the research scope, then use robot-talks as a whole-system fanout over Arcanum and Tandem before synthesizing integration options.

route_menu.context_hint: The strategy chooses whole-system concern fanout because the prior dispatch-spec-centered route was too narrow.

items.label: Whole-system concern fanout
items.description: Run independent agents over identity/authority, capability/workflow models, runtime enforcement, memory/evidence, governance, and integration architecture.

items.label: Capability/runtime crosswalk
items.description: Start with a field and concept crosswalk between Arcanum artifacts and Tandem runtime/workflow primitives.

items.label: Toy integration first
items.description: Design a tiny proof workflow after the conceptual comparison is evidence-backed.

steps.name: Recover Arcanum and Tandem source context
stop_conditions: Block if Tandem cannot be inspected locally or remotely.
Flag if Arcanum evidence is limited to a single Formulae package.

steps.name: Run whole-system concern research subagents
convergence_criteria: Each agent cites evidence from both repositories.
Each agent distinguishes whole-Arcanum findings from dispatch-spec-specific findings.
Each agent separates complementary, overlapping, divergent, and anti-candidate integration claims.
stop_conditions: Block synthesis if any critical claim lacks repository evidence.
Defer implementation recommendations until the synthesis gate.

steps.name: Synthesize complements, overlaps, and divergences
convergence_criteria: Name at least two overlaps that require authority decisions.

steps.name: Rank possible whole-system interaction shapes
convergence_criteria: Choose crosswalk, toy workflow, runtime adapter spike, artifact-contract bridge, memory/evidence bridge, or governance decision record.
Record rejected paths and why they are premature.
stop_conditions: Ask the operator before code implementation.
Block any route that collapses Arcanum lifecycle authority into Tandem runtime policy without an explicit bridge contract.
Block any route that treats Tandem runtime evidence as canonical Arcanum knowledge without Arcanum promotion review.

gates.condition: Every finding must cite concrete evidence from whole Arcanum and Tandem source files.
The investigation must include Arcanum framework, registries, sigils, spells, observability, experiments, and runtime adapters rather than only dispatch-spec.
Research outputs may propose candidate mappings but must not promote glossary, ontology, sigil, spell, memory, or runtime authority.
The operator chooses the first proof artifact before implementation starts.
The route must preserve the distinction between Arcanum lifecycle governance and Tandem runtime enforcement.

promotion_guardrails: Do not promote Tandem concepts into Arcanum vocabulary without owner review.
Do not modify Tandem source or Arcanum runtime surfaces during research.
Do not claim schema or runtime compatibility before a field-level crosswalk and authority-boundary decision exist.
Do not treat dispatch-spec as the whole of Arcanum.
Do not treat Tandem runtime evidence as canonical Arcanum knowledge without promotion review.
